summary:
Claim: Supplementary retention on an abutment tooth is primarily enabled by undercut formation through shoulder preparation.  

Evidence: - Undercuts provide superior mechanical interlock due to their ability to resist dislodging forces under occlusal load. - Clinical guidelines consistently recommend shoulder preparations with well-defined margins to create reliable undercuts for retention.  

Answer: C
